page: Amazon Web Services inf2.24xlarge is an instance within the inf2 family, powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor operating at 3.6 GHz. The system provides 96 dedicated vCPUs across 48 physical cores with multi-threading enabled. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and the GPU count is zero. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 50 Gbps.

In benchmark evaluations, the inf2.24xlarge demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, placing in the top 10% for both stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU tests. Memory performance is also highly rated, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top 10% and cached small-block read bandwidth reaching the top 25% at 5929.9 GB/sec. Large-block uncached memory read and write speeds remain in the average tier. For specialized workloads, the instance achieves top-tier prompt processing speeds of 3801.11 tokens/sec for small language models, though text generation and larger model processing fall into the average performance tier.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the inf2.24xlarge offers high resource density with its 96 dedicated vCPUs and top-tier multi-core processing capabilities, making it efficient for compute-heavy parallel tasks. However, the lack of local storage requires external storage attachments, which may introduce latency and additional configuration tradeoffs. This instance is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, software compilation, database operations, static web serving, and CPU-based machine learning inference workloads.
description: Amazon Web Services inf2.24xlarge is a high-capacity instance featuring 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz, paired with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU and memory performance, ranking in the top 10% in PassMark and stress-ng benchmarks. While it lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs, it provides a robust 50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance excels in parallel processing, showing strong performance in database operations, software compilation, and small-model prompt processing. It is highly suited for compute-intensive, multi-threaded workloads, and general-purpose applications requiring substantial memory and processing density.
og_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.24xlarge is a Nitro-based instance featuring 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB DDR4 RAM, and 50 Gbps network bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core CPU and memory performance.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.24xlarge features 96 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 50 Gbps bandwidth, delivering top-tier multi-core performance.
tagline: A high-density compute platform delivering ninety-six dedicated cores and top-tier multi-threaded performance for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor at 3.6 GHz
384.0 GB of DDR4 system memory with a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
Top-tier multi-core CPU and memory performance in benchmark testing
50 Gbps baseline network bandwidth for high-throughput data transfer
Optimized for multi-threaded applications, database operations, and software compilation
